pattern: The ordering of a search tree means that if a node's value is below the lower bound its entire left side is too, and above the upper bound its entire right side is too, so whole branches can be skipped at once.
hints: When a node's value is too small, nothing in its left subtree can survive, so the answer lives somewhere to its right.
Symmetrically, a value above the high bound discards its right subtree, leaving only its left side to consider.
Recurse: if the value is below low return the trimmed right child, if above high return the trimmed left child, otherwise keep the node and trim both children.

edge_inputs.description: trimming removes a value below the lower bound
the root itself is replaced and structure preserved
single node inside the range survives
single node outside the range is removed entirely
low and high collapse to a single permitted value
right-skewed chain trimmed from both ends
bounds cover everything so the tree is unchanged

stress.description: random BST from a shuffled permutation with random in-range bounds
stress.note: bounds are clamped to low<=high within the value range
